sections.paragraph:
προβατικός, ή, όν pert. to (a) sheep (PCairGoodsp 30 VI, 5; XXXI, 9 al. [II a.d.]; 2 Esdr [s. below]) ἡ προβατική (sc. πύλη, as Life of Polycarp by Pionius in the phrase ἐπὶ τὴν καλουμένην Ἐφεσιακήν [Lghtf. II/3 p. 434 n. on ln. 23; s. also p. 450 n. on related phrase in ch. 20 p. 451 ln. 49]. The Christian POxy 1151 [V a.d.]=PGM II p. 212 P 5b, 7f and the ostrakon 3 p. 234, 1 add κολυμβήθρα: sheep pool) sheep gate, a gate in the north city wall of Jerusalem ([ἡ] πύλη ἡ προβατική 2 Esdr 13:1, 32; 22: 39; s. HGuthe, RE VIII 680, 24ff) J 5:2 (s. Hdb. ad loc. and on Βηθζαθά; also JJeremias, D. Wiederentdeckung v. Bethesda ’49).—M-M.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

PCairGoodsp PCairGoodsp = EJGoodspeed, ed., Greek Papyri from the Cairo Museum—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

  1. ln. = line
  2. ch. = chapter(s)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 865
